bead.title: durable-audit commit: scope the git command deadline to execution only (after lock acquisition); never SIGKILL git under contention
bead.description:
PROBLEM: Even with the classifier fix (B1=ddx-83361480) reclassifying "signal: killed" as transient, the underlying defect remains: the durable-audit commit can be forcibly SIGKILLed mid-`git commit` by a command deadline that competes with the shared-lock wait — the worst possible moment to kill git (risk of a wedged .git/index.lock + wasted work). The commit should WAIT/back off for the lock and only deadline the individual git subprocess actually running, not the entire critical section including lock acquisition.

ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:87-89 creates one context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second) covering the whole commitDurableAuditOutputsLocked body, and CommitDurableAuditOutputs (durable_audit.go:49-53) acquires the shared lock via withTrackerLock whose own wait budget is DefaultLockRetryPolicy.MaxElapsed = 30s (cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:57). These two 30s budgets run concurrently, so under contention the command deadline can fire while a git child is mid-flight -> SIGKILL.

PROPOSED FIX (structural — operator-preferred over merely lengthening the deadline): In commitDurableAuditOutputsLocked (durable_audit.go:87), start the command-deadline context AFTER the lock is held, so the deadline measures only git execution time, not lock-wait time (scope a fresh per-command deadline, or begin a single deadline at lock-acquisition). On expiry it returns an error B1 classifies transient. Confirm + assert idempotent resume across a killed-and-retried commit (re-derives dirty paths durable_audit.go:98-104, --only commits durable_audit.go:122). (Fallback if you want a minimal diff: just lengthen the single deadline to comfortably outlast a 30s lock wait + commit — smaller change but leaves the kill-primitive in place; B2 is scoped to the structural fix.)

NON-SCOPE: the classifier (B1); lock retry policy curve; spec text (B3).
IN-SCOPE FILES: cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go, cli/internal/agent/durable_audit_test.go.
bead.acceptance:
1. New test TestCommitDurableAuditOutputs_ConcurrentLockHolderRetriesAndRecovers: in a fixture repo (testutils.NewFixtureRepo standard), dirty a managed path, hold .ddx/.git-tracker.lock from a second goroutine past a shortened command deadline then release; assert the call either waits-and-commits or returns a transient-classified error, and a follow-up CommitDurableAuditOutputs commits the staged state cleanly (git status --short on managed pathspecs is empty).
2. New test TestCommitDurableAuditOutputs_ResumesAfterPartialKill: simulate a killed commit (managed paths staged, HEAD unmoved), call CommitDurableAuditOutputs again, assert clean commit and no-op on a third call (idempotent).
3. Assert the git-subprocess command deadline does not start until the lock is acquired (the command deadline budget is unaffected by lock-wait time).
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestCommitDurableAuditOutputs' -count=1 green.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/gitlock/... -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
